meta.name: Lean Proof Formalization
meta.description: Formalizes informal equational theory proofs into Lean 4, following Terence Tao's skeleton-then-fill process.
meta.author: Southbridge AI

hank.name: Step 1: Analyze & Plan
hank.description: Read the informal proof, create a formalization plan, write equation definitions

command.run: which lake || (echo 'ERROR: lake not found. Install elan first: curl https://raw.githubusercontent.com/leanprover/elan/master/elan-init.sh -sSf | sh' && exit 1)
mkdir -p workspace && cp read_only_data_source/*.md workspace/ && cp read_only_data_source/*.md .

cd lean-project && if [ -d "$HOME/.hankweave-lean-cache/.lake" ]; then echo 'Using cached .lake via symlink'; ln -s "$HOME/.hankweave-lean-cache/.lake" .lake; else echo 'No local cache found, downloading Mathlib...'; lake exe cache get 2>&1 | tail -5; echo 'Saving cache to ~/.hankweave-lean-cache for future runs'; mkdir -p "$HOME/.hankweave-lean-cache" && cp -r .lake "$HOME/.hankweave-lean-cache/.lake"; fi

hank.name: Step 2: Create Skeleton
hank.description: Write lemma statements with sorry proofs, verify they compile

hank.name: Step 3: Fill Proofs
hank.description: Replace sorry placeholders with actual proofs, verifying compilation after each
hank.exhaustWithPrompt: Check sentinel-notes/latest-warning.md for any warnings from the stuck detector. If it flagged circular behavior or being stuck, consider its suggestions. Then check if there are any remaining `sorry` in lean-project/Formalization/Basic.lean. If so, try to fill them — try a DIFFERENT approach than before. Run `lake build` from lean-project/ to verify. If all sorries are filled and compilation succeeds, you are done.

hank.name: Step 4: Validate & Package
hank.description: Final compilation check, count sorries, write output files

command.run: rm -f lean-project/.lake && rm -f lean-project/lake-manifest.json && echo 'Cleaned .lake symlink'
